features.summary: P&G delivered fiscal 2024 results that met or exceeded all guidance ranges with 4% organic sales growth, 12% core EPS growth, and 105% cash productivity. Fourth quarter organic sales grew 2% with volume up 2%, showing sequential improvement. Growth was broad-based across categories and regions, though headwinds persisted in China (down 8%) and some enterprise markets. The company maintained strong margins with core gross margin at a 17-year high while significantly increasing marketing investments.
features.management_outlook: Management provided fiscal 2025 guidance of 3-5% organic sales growth and 5-7% core EPS growth, consistent with long-term algorithms. They expect markets to grow 3-4% with roughly balanced volume and price-mix contributions. The outlook assumes annualization of current headwinds in China, Middle East, and other challenged markets by the second half, with no material improvement assumed. Management emphasized confidence in their integrated strategy and ability to deliver balanced growth despite ongoing volatility.
features.risks: Key risks include continued weakness in China markets and SK-II brand performance, ongoing geopolitical tensions affecting Middle East operations, and commodity cost headwinds of approximately $300 million. Foreign exchange presents a $200 million headwind, and the company faces difficult comparisons in the first half of fiscal 2025. Consumer pressure remains a watch item, though not currently evident in P&G's categories.
features.key_drivers: Growth drivers include strong innovation pipelines across categories, continued investment in marketing and brand building, and productivity improvements enabling reinvestment. North America showed consistent 4% growth with volume acceleration, while Europe focus markets delivered strong performance. The company's focus on superiority across all five vectors (product, package, communication, retail execution, value) and supply chain 3.0 initiatives are expected to drive future performance.
